text:
Once upon a time, in a quaint village nestled between rolling hills and an enchanting forest, lived an old bookworm named Harold. Harold spent every moment he wasn't buried in a tome on forgotten myths and legends, exploring the rich tapestry of human stories.

One brisk autumn day, while harvesting apples from his beloved orchard, Harold stumbled upon an old, crumbling tome hidden beneath a pile of leaves. As he carefully lifted it out, he noticed it was far bigger than any other book he'd found. Intrigued, he opened it. The pages were covered in glittering, hand-lettered script, and within the book, instead of words, were strange, intricate drawings.

Excited by the discovery, Harold returned to his study. He spent hours deciphering the meanings behind the pictures, discovering lost tales of heroes, gods, and mythical creatures. He imagined themselves in the scrolls, feeling their weight in his hands.
